Fern Por-rrrcAr- PnACTrcEs CoMMrssroN
.ll8 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento. CA g_58 l.l-1.129
(916) -122-5660 o Fax (9t6) 322-0886
June26,2O09
George Watson
Chief of Staff for
Third District Supervisor Neil Derry
County Government Center
385 N. Arrowhead Ave.
San Bemardino, Califo mia 924 I 5
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-137
Dear Mr. Watson:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the economic interest
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QIIESTTON
Are you required to report money on your statement of economic interests that
you won from participation in an online poker tournament when the online poker
provider is physically located overseas but does business over the Internet?
CONCLUSION
Yes. As discussed in more detail below, you are required to report as income on
your statement of economic interests money you won from participation in an online
poker toumament when the online poker provider is physically located overseas but does
business over the lnternet.
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Secrions 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through ltt997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-09-137
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are a chief of staff for a San Bemardino County Supervisor, having been
hired to this position on April 11,2009. You are a designated employee in your agincy's
conflict of interest code. Your position is listed as category 'i," whictr requiies fuil
disclosure. In late March, you won $9,500 in an online poker tournament thrbugh Full
Tilt Poker. You also won gl,l00 in another toumament. Before and after youihiring
date, you played in online poker tournaments where you did not win money but lost the
cost of your entry fee. You believe Full Tilt Poker operates on foreign soii but does its
business over the Internet. You would like to know if and in whit manner you are
required to report proceeds won in online poker tournaments when the online company is
located out of the jurisdiction but does business through the Intemet. You would like to
know if you are required to file an amendment to your statement of economic interests
each time you win and if you are required to report your net winnings after all wins and
losses.
AIIALYSIS
Section 87300 requires that every agency adopt and promulgate a conflict of
interest code. Those required to file statements of economic interesis pursuant to
categories specified in an agency's conflict of interest code must r"port income of $500
or more from the sources identified in the conflict of interest code. lsection 87302.)
with some exceptions, the Act defines income as "a payment received . . ..', Income,
other than a gift, does not include income received from any source outside the
jurisdiction and not doing business within the jurisdiction, not planning to do business
within the jurisdiction, or not having done business within the jurisdictlon during the two
years prior to the time any statement or other action is required. (Section 82030.) A
person is "doing business in the jurisdiction" if that person has business contacts on a
regular or substantial basis with a person who maintains a physical presence in the
jurisdiction of a public official. "Business contacts" include, but ari not limited to,
manufacturing, distributing, selling, purchasing, or providing services or goods.
(Regulation 18230.)
You are required to file a statement of economic interests under your agency,s
conflict of interest code and, therefore, you are required to report income you riceive.
You indicated that you believe Full Tilt Poker is an online company that operates on
foreign soil but does business on the Intemet. This however does not mean they are
outside of your jurisdiction for purposes of the Act and its reporting requirements.
As you stated in your request for advice, you have played in online poker toumaments
through the Full Tilt Poker site on multiple occasions. This activityqualifies as
"substantial" contact with someone who has a physical presence in your jurisdiction.
Because the funds you win through online poker tournaments are "paymlnts received,,,
they do not fall under any of the exceptions to income listed in Section 87302(b),and the
online provider is doing business in the jurisdiction, these payments are income to you.


File No. A-09-137
Page No. 3
Therefore, because you are a designated employee who receives income as it is
defined under the Act, you are required to report this income on your statement of
economic interests.2 You must report all of your winnings without respect to your losses
in the tournaments. You are required to file a statement of economic interests annually,
which would not require you to update your statements each time you win money. you
are required to report the total of all of your winnings of $500 or more from each source
for the year you are filing your report without subtracting your losses. The deadline for
filing statements of economic interests for any given year is after that year has passed so
updates would not be required once you have filed your statement of economisinterests
unless you later find that you have made a mistake that requires correction.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
J&U" /-nw^,'*
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
SKBjgl
2 Though you must report your winnings fiom the online poker tournaments as income on your
statement of economic interests, our advice is limited to the requirements of the Act and we do not provide
advice as to the legality of participation in such online poker tournaments.

Yes. As discussed in more detail below, you are required to report as income on
your statement of economic interests money you won from participation in an online
poker toumament when the online poker provider is physically located overseas but does
business over the lnternet.
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Secrions 81000 through 91014. The regulations of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through ltt997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
